div.head: The American Delegate (Wilson) to the Secretary of State
div.p: 504. As you know there is to be a general discussion of the French planFor text, see telegram No. 455, November 15, 1932, from the American delegate, Foreign Relations, 1932, vol. i, p. 380. in the early stages of the meeting of the General Commission which has been set for January 31. Inasmuch as the French plan involves for us some thorny political questions, notably consultation and neutral rights, it would seem advisable to consider as soon as possible what form any observation of the American delegation should take in discussing the plan. It would appear that any pronouncement we might make would be of considerable political importance either through what is said or through what it omitted to say. Thus it would seem advantageous to enter into an exchange of views with you as soon as possible regarding the matter.
